content.full_text:
Memo re telephone advice: Staff: Margaret Ellison

To: Craig Primo, Campaign Manager, McClintock Committee

Tel. No.: (805) 495-0405

Date: 8/22/89

I called Mr. Primo in response to his letter of August 9, 1989, requesting written confirmation of the effective date of the Taxpayers decision. I told him that the Commission would be considering an opinion request and possibly rendering an opinion at the 9/6/89 meeting. I told him that there was also a possibility of clarification from the Court of Appeal in response to our request for rehearing to clarify that issue. I advised him that we would not be responding in writing to requests for an effective date, because of the anticipated Commission opinion which would serve as written advice for everyone. I stated that based upon information received from the clerk's office and our subsequent research, we believed the earliest enforcement date to be 10/3/89.

Post Office Box 6336
Thousand Oaks, California 91362
(805) 495-0405

T89-19-506
Committees:
Ways and Means
Judiciary
Revenue and Taxation
Education
Policy Research
Member, Republican
Policy Committee
Member, Republican
Task Force on
Tort Reform

Assembly
California Legislature

THOMAS McCLINTOCK
ASSEMBLYMAN, THIRTY-SIXTH DISTRICT

Assembly Republican Whip
Vice Chairman
Assembly Judiciary Committee

August 9, 1989

FPPC
attn: John Larson
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Larson:

I am writing to you today requesting written confirmation of the
FPPC's position on a recent court ruling. The decision in
question is the one handed down last week in the 2nd district
count of appeals regarding off-year contributions. My question
to you is this: what is the effective date of this decision.
Thank you for your time.

Sincerely,

Craig Primo
Campaign Manager

Paid for by McClintock Committee
Walter Knous, Treasurer, I.D. #50776

Not Printed or Mailed at Taxpayer's Expense
